title: Turku Halal Lamb Farm
summary: A halal-certified lamb farm supplying mosques and families across Southwest Finland.
description: A family-run farm outside Turku is pooling capital to scale halal-certified lamb production for the local Muslim community. Funds cover certified slaughtering facilities, cold storage, and a refrigerated van for weekly delivery to mosques and halal grocers in Turku and Helsinki. The farm already supplies four community shops and aims to cut prices for families during Eid.

title: Helsinki Modest Fashion Atelier
summary: A modest-wear label and tailoring studio employing women from the local community.
description: A Helsinki atelier designing and producing modest clothing — abayas, hijabs, and modest workwear — for the Nordic market. Capital funds sewing equipment, a small storefront in Itäkeskus, and paid training for women re-entering work. A 12% revenue share funds a community sewing class.

title: Espoo Islamic Centre Solar Roof
summary: Community-owned solar array cutting the Espoo mosque's energy bills to near zero.
description: A 180 kW rooftop solar installation plus battery storage, owned collectively by the congregation of the Espoo Islamic Centre. Savings on heating and electricity are redirected into the centre's youth and weekend-school programmes. Pre-assessed by a Finnish renewable-energy installer.

title: Tampere Halal Food Co-op
summary: A member-owned halal grocery and butcher serving the Tampere community.
description: Twelve families in Tampere are opening a cooperative halal grocery and butcher, buying directly from Finnish halal farms to keep prices fair. Funds cover the shop fit-out, refrigeration, and first stock. Members share in the surplus and vote on what the shop stocks.

title: Helsinki Halal Eats App
summary: A delivery platform connecting Finnish halal restaurants and home cooks.
description: A mobile platform that lets the community discover and order from verified halal restaurants and licensed home kitchens across Helsinki. The team has signed twenty restaurants and charges a low flat fee, with reduced commission for community-run kitchens.

title: Vantaa Community Centre & Prayer Hall
summary: A multipurpose centre with a prayer hall, youth space, and women's programmes.
description: Acquiring and fitting out a building in Vantaa as a community centre: a prayer hall, classrooms for weekend school, a youth room, and a hall for weddings and funerals. Designed as a shared, transparent community asset funded by the people who use it.

title: Oulu Halal Bakery & Café
summary: A neighbourhood bakery and café employing newcomer families in Oulu.
description: A bakery and café in Oulu making fresh bread, Nordic-Middle Eastern pastries, and halal lunches, with paid kitchen training for recently arrived families. A 3% share funds Finnish-language classes for staff.

title: Turku Halal Logistics Hub
summary: A temperature-controlled halal distribution hub serving Finnish grocers.
description: An early-stage plan for a shared cold-chain warehouse near Turku that keeps halal supply chains separated and certified end-to-end, serving community grocers across Finland. Funds would cover the first 18 months of operations and certification before any scale-up.

title: Lahti Date & Olive Importer
summary: Direct-import dates and olive oil for Finnish Muslim families, fairly priced.
description: A small importer in Lahti bringing dates, olive oil, and pantry staples directly from farming cooperatives to Finnish Muslim households, cutting out middlemen. Funds cover the first container orders, storage, and an online shop for the Nordics.
